brief.name: drishtikon-foundation-lms
brief.description: A production-grade, accessible platform for visually impaired learners featuring a public portal, a secure role-based Learning Management System, and an administrative CMS with full operations panels.
features: Public website with programs, staff profiles, and donation module
Role-based access control (RBAC) with JWT authentication
Student dashboard with enrollment tracking and accessible materials
Teacher dashboard for lesson planning and test creation
Staff dashboard for mobility/life-skill session reporting
Admin panel for user management and curriculum oversight
Weekly assessments with MCQ and descriptive grading workflows
WCAG 2.1 AA compliant keyboard navigation and screen-reader support
Audit logging for critical security actions
brief.raw_text:
You are a principal full-stack engineer. Build a real, production-style platform (not a demo) for Drishtikon Foundation.

Project:
Drishtikon Foundation serves visually impaired learners. Build:
1) Public website
2) Secure role-based LMS
3) Admin CMS + operations panel

Hard stack constraints (do not change):
- Backend: Python + FastAPI
- ORM: SQLAlchemy 2.x
- Migrations: Alembic
- Database: SQLite for now (via DATABASE_URL), but architecture must be Postgres-ready later
- Frontend: Server-rendered templates + static HTML/CSS/JS (accessible, responsive)
- Auth: JWT (access + refresh) with RBAC
- Tests: pytest

Core users/roles:
- public
- student
- teacher
- staff
- admin
- parent (optional but included if feasible)

Must-have product modules:

Public portal:
- Home, About, Mission & Vision, Programs, Course Overview, Staff Profiles, Success Stories, Gallery, Annual Reports, Contact, Volunteer Registration, Donation

Learning system:
- Programs: Fundamental Computer, Mobility Training, Spoken English, Life Skills
- Program -> Course -> Module -> Lesson hierarchy
- Accessible materials (PDF/DOCX/audio links)
- Weekly assessments for modules

Student dashboard:
- Profile
- Enrollments + progress
- Materials access/download
- Weekly tests + timed submission
- Results + progress analytics
- Attendance history
- Notifications

Teacher dashboard:
- Lesson planning + material upload metadata
- Test creation (MCQ + descriptive)
- Grading workflows
- Publish results
- Attendance management
- Student performance overview

Staff dashboard:
- Support tracking
- Mobility/life-skill session reports
- Attendance monitoring
- Internal communication notes
- Reports

Admin dashboard:
- User management
- Course/program/curriculum management
- Test approval + monitoring
- Result verification
- Basic analytics
- CMS content management
- Backup metadata + audit log viewer

Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA, mandatory):
- Full keyboard navigation
- Skip-to-content link
- ARIA labels/landmarks
- Proper form labeling + error associations
- High contrast toggle
- Font size controls
- Dyslexia-friendly font option
- Screen-reader friendly charts/tables (text equivalents)
- No inaccessible CAPTCHA

Security requirements:
- RBAC enforced on API and UI routes
- Password hashing (bcrypt/argon2)
- Input validation with Pydantic
- SQL injection-safe ORM usage
- CSRF protection for form-based state-changing endpoints
- Secure token handling
- Audit logs for auth/admin critical actions

SQLite-now, Postgres-later rules:
- Use SQLAlchemy models and Alembic migrations cleanly
- Avoid SQLite-specific hacks in business logic
- Store datetimes in UTC
- Prefer UUID/string-safe IDs for portability
- Keep DB access behind repository/service boundaries

Minimum data model entities:
- users, roles, student_profiles, teacher_profiles, staff_profiles, parent_links
- programs, courses, modules, lessons, materials
- enrollments, attendance_sessions, attendance_records
- assessments, questions, options, submissions, answers, results
- notifications, announcements
- volunteers, donations
- content_pages (CMS)
- audit_logs

API requirements:
- Versioned API prefix (/api/v1)
- Auth endpoints
- Role-specific dashboard endpoints
- Course/module/lesson/material CRUD (role-restricted)
- Assessment lifecycle endpoints
- Submission + grading endpoints
- Attendance endpoints
- Notification endpoints
- Public content endpoints
- Admin analytics summary endpoints

UI quality bar:
- Professional, cohesive design system
- Fully responsive (mobile/tablet/desktop)
- No lorem ipsum, no fake placeholders, no empty dashboard cards
- Real loading, error, empty states
- Real data bindings to backend

Testing requirements:
- Unit tests for services/repositories
- API integration tests for auth + RBAC + assessments
- Accessibility smoke checks for key pages
- Migration test (fresh DB + upgrade head)

Deliverables:
- Complete runnable codebase
- Alembic migrations
- Seed script with realistic sample data
- README with setup/run/test commands
- Admin bootstrap instructions
- Architecture summary and endpoint list

Definition of done:
- App runs end-to-end on SQLite
- All major role workflows are functional
- Weekly test flow works (create -> attempt -> submit -> grade -> publish)
- Accessibility and security requirements are implemented
- Test suite passes
